<findings>

[observation] [info] CLAUDE.md's two-consumer rule and framework-agnostic-only policy are the right guardrails; ensure they are enforced at PR review time, not just documented.
[observation] [warning] Cohesion of 0.36 is below the 0.4 warning threshold — the two exported utilities (data-files and view-id) share little internal relationship, making the zone feel artificially bundled.
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.64 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold — five inbound import edges from web-viewer alone make this the highest-traffic foundation layer in the web package.
[pattern] [warning] The cohesion degradation from the CLAUDE.md baseline (0.46 → 0.36) is measurable and ongoing; the two-consumer rule is documented but not machine-enforced by boundary-check.test.ts, leaving the primary governance at PR review discretion only.
[pattern] [warning] web-shared cohesion has degraded from 0.46 (documented in CLAUDE.md) to 0.36 while coupling increased from 0.54 to 0.64. The zone has 5 files now vs the 3 mentioned in the overview context — the discrepancy suggests recent file additions violated the two-consumer rule or added framework-specific utilities. Audit data-files.ts, view-id.ts, and node-culler.ts for single-consumer additions.
[anti-pattern] [warning] The two-consumer rule for web-shared additions has no automated enforcement in boundary-check.test.ts despite being documented as an active governance policy. Cohesion has degraded from the 0.46 documented baseline to 0.36 (a 22% drop), confirming that PR review alone is insufficient. Add a consumer-count assertion that reads the actual import graph and fails if any web-shared module has fewer than two distinct consumer zones.
[anti-pattern] [warning] web-shared's cohesion degradation (0.46→0.36) originates from export sprawl within data-files.ts or view-id.ts rather than file additions. The two files export symbols that share no conceptual relationship (file path constants vs UI view identifiers), making the zone artificially bundled with no unifying abstraction. Splitting data-files.ts and view-id.ts into separate zones — each with its own consumer set — would restore cohesion and make the two-consumer rule enforceable per-module rather than per-zone.
[suggestion] [info] Zone name 'web-shared' is the least descriptive name in the monorepo zone map — 'shared' communicates placement but not purpose. The zone exports file-path constants (DATA_FILES) and a UI identifier type (ViewId), which are unrelated enough that separate zone names ('web-data-constants', 'web-view-id') would better reflect the actual content and make the two-consumer rule enforceable per-module rather than per-zone.
[suggestion] [warning] shared/index.ts is the only cohesion mechanism in this zone — it is a barrel exporting 3 constants and 1 type from two semantically unrelated files. No automated check enforces that consumers import through index.ts rather than directly from data-files.ts or view-id.ts. If direct imports proliferate, zone cohesion becomes unmeasurable and the two-consumer rule cannot be applied per-module. boundary-check.test.ts should assert that web-shared consumers import through the barrel index rather than from leaf files directly.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "Web Shared Foundation" (web-shared) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — unreliable: zone has only 3 files (minimum 5 for reliable metrics)

</findings>

<insights>

- Both cohesion (0.36) and coupling (0.64) breach their respective thresholds simultaneously, matching CLAUDE.md's designation of this as the only production zone in dual-fragility territory.
- Three consumer zones (web-viewer, web-server, viewer-message-pipeline) import from this zone, making any change here a wide blast-radius event.
- The two-consumer rule in CLAUDE.md is the primary governance tool; additions should be reviewed against it before merging.
- Cohesion of 0.36 is below the 0.4 warning threshold — the two exported utilities (data-files and view-id) share little internal relationship, making the zone feel artificially bundled.
- Coupling of 0.64 exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold — five inbound import edges from web-viewer alone make this the highest-traffic foundation layer in the web package.
- CLAUDE.md's two-consumer rule and framework-agnostic-only policy are the right guardrails; ensure they are enforced at PR review time, not just documented.
- web-shared receives imports from two zones (web-viewer: 5, web-unit: 1) with zero reverse edges — a perfect unidirectional foundation pattern. This confirms the two-consumer rule is satisfied and the zone is not creating upward dependencies.
- With cohesion now at 0.36 (down from 0.46 documented in CLAUDE.md), the zone's fragility has worsened despite satisfying the consumer count threshold. Cohesion degradation without adding consumers suggests existing modules are becoming more loosely related internally, not just externally coupled.
- web-shared cohesion has degraded from 0.46 (documented in CLAUDE.md) to 0.36 while coupling increased from 0.54 to 0.64. The zone has 5 files now vs the 3 mentioned in the overview context — the discrepancy suggests recent file additions violated the two-consumer rule or added framework-specific utilities. Audit data-files.ts, view-id.ts, and node-culler.ts for single-consumer additions.
- The cohesion degradation from the CLAUDE.md baseline (0.46 → 0.36) is measurable and ongoing; the two-consumer rule is documented but not machine-enforced by boundary-check.test.ts, leaving the primary governance at PR review discretion only.
- boundary-check.test.ts has no automated assertion for the two-consumer rule. The policy is documented in CLAUDE.md and enforced only by PR review, yet cohesion has measurably degraded from the 0.46 baseline to 0.36 — evidence that review alone has not held the line. The gap between documented policy and test-enforced policy is the root cause of the observed degradation.
- The two-consumer rule for web-shared additions has no automated enforcement in boundary-check.test.ts despite being documented as an active governance policy. Cohesion has degraded from the 0.46 documented baseline to 0.36 (a 22% drop), confirming that PR review alone is insufficient. Add a consumer-count assertion that reads the actual import graph and fails if any web-shared module has fewer than two distinct consumer zones.
- web-shared's cohesion degradation (0.46→0.36) originates from export sprawl within data-files.ts or view-id.ts rather than file additions. The two files export symbols that share no conceptual relationship (file path constants vs UI view identifiers), making the zone artificially bundled with no unifying abstraction. Splitting data-files.ts and view-id.ts into separate zones — each with its own consumer set — would restore cohesion and make the two-consumer rule enforceable per-module rather than per-zone.
- packages/web/src/shared/index.ts is the sole cohesion mechanism for this zone — it is a pure barrel that re-exports DATA_FILES/ALL_DATA_FILES/SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES from data-files.ts and ViewId from view-id.ts. If any consumer bypasses index.ts and imports directly from data-files.ts or view-id.ts, the zone loses its only internal connective tissue, making cohesion enforcement entirely dependent on import discipline rather than module structure.
- The shared/index.ts barrel exports only 3 constants and 1 type across 2 unrelated source files. This is the thinnest possible barrel interface — 4 total exports from 2 conceptually unrelated modules — confirming that the zone is held together by the barrel alone, not by any genuine shared abstraction.
- The zone name 'web-shared' uses a package-prefix+role naming pattern, but its role ('shared') is the least descriptive possible term for a foundation zone. Other foundation zones in the monorepo use function-specific names (llm-client, rex-gateway). Renaming to 'web-foundation' or 'web-constants' would align with the zone's actual content (framework-agnostic constants and a view identifier type).
- shared/index.ts is the only cohesion mechanism in this zone — it is a barrel exporting 3 constants and 1 type from two semantically unrelated files. No automated check enforces that consumers import through index.ts rather than directly from data-files.ts or view-id.ts. If direct imports proliferate, zone cohesion becomes unmeasurable and the two-consumer rule cannot be applied per-module. boundary-check.test.ts should assert that web-shared consumers import through the barrel index rather than from leaf files directly.
- Zone name 'web-shared' is the least descriptive name in the monorepo zone map — 'shared' communicates placement but not purpose. The zone exports file-path constants (DATA_FILES) and a UI identifier type (ViewId), which are unrelated enough that separate zone names ('web-data-constants', 'web-view-id') would better reflect the actual content and make the two-consumer rule enforceable per-module rather than per-zone.

</insights>
